title: Repository scaffolding
description: Deliverable of M0: Architecture and protocol foundation. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: Monorepo tree, go.mod/go.work, README/LICENSE/SECURITY/CONTRIBUTING, .gitignore. Verified: go build, go vet, gofmt clean.

title: M2: Knowledge and task foundation
description: Dolt knowledge schema, provenance model, Beads integration, requirement-to-task mapping, cross-repository relations. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
acceptance_criteria: A requirement can link to files and tests across repositories; every relation retains source provenance; Beads tasks map back to requirements and Jira keys; knowledge changes can be diffed and versioned.

title: M1: Core runtime and local repository workflow
description: CLI, workspace discovery, policy engine, approval storage, tool supervisor, Git worktree lifecycle, JSONL event journal. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
acceptance_criteria: Punakawan opens a workspace with multiple repositories; it can inspect repositories without writing; it can create an isolated worktree after approval; every command is auditable and cancellable; default-branch writes are blocked.

title: M0: Architecture and protocol foundation
description: Repository scaffolding, Go/pnpm workspaces, canonical JSON Schema protocol, JSON-RPC stdio prototype. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
acceptance_criteria: Go can start a TypeScript adapter; both sides validate the same message schemas; cancellation and timeout work; CI detects stale generated code.

title: M5: Playwright human-guided recorder
description: Playwright adapter, controlled headed-browser mode, recorder injection, semantic locator generation, sensitive-input sanitizer. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
acceptance_criteria: A user can demonstrate a flow; Punakawan stores URLs, clicks, and sanitized inputs; passwords and tokens are never stored; the normalized flow survives minor DOM layout changes; the flow can link to a requirement and E2E repository.

title: M4: Existing document and Atlassian integrations
description: Docling adapter, Atlassian adapter, Jira/Confluence search and read, draft comment generation, approval-gated writes. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
acceptance_criteria: Punakawan imports a Jira issue and supporting Confluence page; extracted knowledge preserves page, section, version, and hash; it detects when a source version changes; external writes cannot occur without policy approval.

title: M3: Semar, Gareng, and Petruk planning workflow
description: Role contracts, prompt templates, context dossier generation, Gareng review, Petruk challenge, Semar clarification synthesis. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
acceptance_criteria: A feature request produces a structured context dossier; missing information is classified as blocking or non-blocking; clarification wording is diplomatic and evidence-backed; the final plan includes unit, integration, E2E, deployment, and documentation impact.

title: M8: Bagong independent review
description: Independent context builder, requirement coverage analysis, diff/test/API/migration/E2E review, finding severity model, reopen-task workflow. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
acceptance_criteria: Bagong reviews raw sources and diffs independently; missing acceptance criteria are visible; high-severity findings reopen or create tasks; delivery cannot be marked complete with unresolved blocking findings.

title: M7: Playwright test generation and adjustment
description: Flow-to-Playwright compiler, parameterization, fixture integration, assertion generation, existing-test matching. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
acceptance_criteria: A recorded flow generates a valid Playwright test; existing tests can be updated rather than duplicated; generated tests use semantic locators; test reports and traces become evidence.

title: M6: Plan-to-Beads and Petruk execution
description: Task graph generator, ready-task selection, worktree executor, diff checker, test execution, OpenAPI compatibility check, discovered-task handling. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
acceptance_criteria: An approved plan becomes dependency-aware tasks; Petruk executes only ready tasks; each task has isolated changes and evidence; scope expansion becomes a new task; failed checks prevent task completion.

title: M9: Hardening and release
description: Recovery after interruption, adapter sandboxing, secret broker, tool installer/checksums, OpenTelemetry, SBOM, signed release artifacts. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
acceptance_criteria: Interrupted runs recover safely; orphan child processes are terminated; tool binaries are checksum-verified; secrets are excluded from logs and evidence; release artifacts work on supported platforms.
